title: classification_result_{document_id}.json -- document type classification, one file per document v0.2
description: Assigns a type label to one document. Produced by document-pipeline's checkpoint 1 (OCR + cross-validation + classification, in one vision-model call per document -- see pipeline.md) -- one file per document (classification_result_{document_id}.json), since checkpoint 1 runs once per document and a single shared file across documents would get silently overwritten by each subsequent document's write (write-contract replaces the whole file; see known-gaps.md's audit of this exact failure mode, first found in ocr_result.json). v0.1 nested everything under a documents: [] array to cover multiple documents in one file -- v0.2 flattens that away since each file now covers exactly one document. Consumed by claim-analysis, policy-pipeline, and denial-response for document selection.

document_type_label.description: Display label in Korean (the language of the source documents). e.g. 진단서, 보험사 안내문

pre_flagged.description: True if this document's type was already known at intake (e.g. a flagged insurer-response document) rather than inferred here. When true, classification skips inference and trusts the flag -- see harness-guardrails-dev and denial-response.md.

candidate_types.description: Candidates in descending confidence order. First entry must match predicted_document_type.

evidence_references.description: Evidence for the classification. document_id may be omitted since document context is already unambiguous.
